# Conventions {.unnumbered}

*Figures throughout this book use a consistent visual language for tensor shapes, dimensions, and components. This page collects those conventions in one place so you can decode any diagram at a glance.*

### Dimension names and abbreviations

Tensors in figures are labeled with their shapes using the single-letter abbreviations below. The full names and abbreviations match those used in the text.

| Symbol | Full name | Example values (Llama 3 70B) |
|:------:|:----------|:-----------------------------|
| **B**              | Batch size | 1--64 |
| **[D]{.dim-d}**    | Model dimension ($d_\text{model}$) | 8192 |
| $\dimdk{d_K}$      | Head dimension ($D / H$) | 128 |
| **[F]{.dim-f}**    | FFN intermediate dimension ($d_\text{ff}$) | 28672 (SwiGLU) |
| **[H]{.dim-h}**    | Number of attention heads ($n_\text{heads}$) | 64 |
| $\dimhkv{H_\text{KV}}$ | Number of KV heads | 8 (with GQA) |
| **[L]{.dim-l}**    | Number of layers | 80 |
| **[S]{.dim-s}**    | Sequence length | Prefill: prompt length (e.g., 2048); Decode: 1 |
| **[V]{.dim-v}**    | Vocabulary size | 128000 |

: Standard dimension symbols used in figures and text. {#tbl-dim-symbols .striped}


### Tensor edge colors

Tensor rectangles use **colored edges** to indicate which dimension runs along that axis. Top/bottom edges represent one dimension; left/right edges represent another. This makes it possible to see at a glance how dimensions flow through a computation.

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
```{=html}
<table style="border-collapse: collapse; margin: 1em 0;">
<caption>Edge colors encode tensor dimensions.</caption>
<thead>
<tr>
  <th style="padding: 8px 16px; border-bottom: 2px solid #333; text-align: center;">Edge</th>
  <th style="padding: 8px 16px; border-bottom: 2px solid #333; text-align: left;">Dimension</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px; text-align: center;">
    <svg width="60" height="6"><line x1="0" y1="3" x2="60" y2="3" stroke="#1E40AF" stroke-width="3"/></svg>
  </td>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px;">Model dimension (<strong><span class="dim-d">D</span></strong>)</td>
</tr>
<tr style="background-color: #f5f5f5;">
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px; text-align: center;">
    <svg width="60" height="6"><line x1="0" y1="3" x2="60" y2="3" stroke="#93C5FD" stroke-width="3"/></svg>
  </td>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px;">Head / Q-K dimension (<strong><span class="dim-dk">\(d_K\)</span></strong>)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px; text-align: center;">
    <svg width="60" height="6"><line x1="0" y1="3" x2="60" y2="3" stroke="#059669" stroke-width="3"/></svg>
  </td>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px;">FFN intermediate dimension (<strong><span class="dim-f">F</span></strong>)</td>
</tr>
<tr style="background-color: #f5f5f5;">
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px; text-align: center;">
    <svg width="60" height="6"><line x1="0" y1="3" x2="60" y2="3" stroke="#A855F7" stroke-width="3"/></svg>
  </td>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px;">Number of attention heads (<strong><span class="dim-h">H</span></strong>)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px; text-align: center;">
    <svg width="60" height="6"><line x1="0" y1="3" x2="60" y2="3" stroke="#C084FC" stroke-width="3"/></svg>
  </td>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px;">Number of KV heads (<strong><span class="dim-hkv">H<sub>KV</sub></span></strong>)</td>
</tr>
<tr style="background-color: #f5f5f5;">
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px; text-align: center;">
    <svg width="60" height="6"><line x1="0" y1="3" x2="60" y2="3" stroke="#8B4513" stroke-width="3"/></svg>
  </td>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px;">Number of layers (<strong><span class="dim-l">L</span></strong>)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px; text-align: center;">
    <svg width="60" height="6"><line x1="0" y1="3" x2="60" y2="3" stroke="#DC2626" stroke-width="3"/></svg>
  </td>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px;">Sequence length (<strong><span class="dim-s">S</span></strong>)</td>
</tr>
<tr style="background-color: #f5f5f5;">
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px; text-align: center;">
    <svg width="60" height="6"><line x1="0" y1="3" x2="60" y2="3" stroke="#CA8A04" stroke-width="3"/></svg>
  </td>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px;">Vocabulary (<strong><span class="dim-v">V</span></strong>)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px; text-align: center;">
    <svg width="60" height="6"><line x1="0" y1="3" x2="60" y2="3" stroke="#1E3A5F" stroke-width="3"/></svg>
  </td>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px;">Default / other</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
```
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="pdf"}
```{=latex}
\begin{center}
\captionof{table}{Edge colors encode tensor dimensions.}
\label{tbl-edge-colors}
\rowcolors{2}{white}{gray!8}
\begin{tabular}{cl}
\toprule
Edge & Dimension \\
\midrule
\textcolor{dim-d}{\rule{1.5cm}{2.5pt}} & Model dimension ($\dimd{\mathbf{D}}$) \\[4pt]
\textcolor{edge-dk}{\rule{1.5cm}{2.5pt}} & Head / Q-K dimension ($\dimdk{\mathbf{d_K}}$) \\[4pt]
\textcolor{dim-f}{\rule{1.5cm}{2.5pt}} & FFN intermediate dimension ($\dimf{\mathbf{F}}$) \\[4pt]
\textcolor{dim-h}{\rule{1.5cm}{2.5pt}} & Number of attention heads ($\dimh{\mathbf{H}}$) \\[4pt]
\textcolor{dim-hkv}{\rule{1.5cm}{2.5pt}} & Number of KV heads ($\dimhkv{\mathbf{H_\text{KV}}}$) \\[4pt]
\textcolor{dim-l}{\rule{1.5cm}{2.5pt}} & Number of layers ($\diml{\mathbf{L}}$) \\[4pt]
\textcolor{dim-s}{\rule{1.5cm}{2.5pt}} & Sequence length ($\dims{\mathbf{S}}$) \\[4pt]
\textcolor{dim-v}{\rule{1.5cm}{2.5pt}} & Vocabulary ($\dimv{\mathbf{V}}$) \\[4pt]
\textcolor{edge-default}{\rule{1.5cm}{2.5pt}} & Default / other \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}
```
:::


### Tensor fill colors

The **fill color** of a tensor rectangle indicates its role in the computation.

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
```{=html}
<table style="border-collapse: collapse; margin: 1em 0;">
<caption>Fill colors encode tensor roles.</caption>
<thead>
<tr>
  <th style="padding: 8px 16px; border-bottom: 2px solid #333; text-align: center;">Fill</th>
  <th style="padding: 8px 16px; border-bottom: 2px solid #333; text-align: left;">Meaning</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px; text-align: center;">
    <svg width="40" height="24"><rect x="0" y="0" width="40" height="24" rx="3" fill="#F3F4F6" stroke="#6B7280" stroke-width="1"/></svg>
  </td>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px;">Input / activations (neutral)</td>
</tr>
<tr style="background-color: #f5f5f5;">
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px; text-align: center;">
    <svg width="40" height="24"><rect x="0" y="0" width="40" height="24" rx="3" fill="#F3E8FF" stroke="#6B7280" stroke-width="1"/></svg>
  </td>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px;">Weight matrices</td>
</tr>
<tr>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px; text-align: center;">
    <svg width="40" height="24"><rect x="0" y="0" width="40" height="24" rx="3" fill="#EFF6FF" stroke="#1E40AF" stroke-width="1"/></svg>
  </td>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px;">Queries (<strong>Q</strong>)</td>
</tr>
<tr style="background-color: #f5f5f5;">
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px; text-align: center;">
    <svg width="40" height="24"><rect x="0" y="0" width="40" height="24" rx="3" fill="#FFF8E1" stroke="#6B7280" stroke-width="1"/></svg>
  </td>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px;">Keys (<strong>K</strong>)</td>
</tr>
<tr>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px; text-align: center;">
    <svg width="40" height="24"><rect x="0" y="0" width="40" height="24" rx="3" fill="#FEE2E2" stroke="#6B7280" stroke-width="1"/></svg>
  </td>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px;">Values (<strong>V</strong>)</td>
</tr>
<tr style="background-color: #f5f5f5;">
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px; text-align: center;">
    <svg width="40" height="24"><rect x="0" y="0" width="40" height="24" rx="3" fill="#D1FAE5" stroke="#6B7280" stroke-width="1"/></svg>
  </td>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px;">Attention scores / weights</td>
</tr>
<tr>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px; text-align: center;">
    <svg width="40" height="24"><rect x="0" y="0" width="40" height="24" rx="3" fill="#FEF3C7" stroke="#6B7280" stroke-width="1"/></svg>
  </td>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px;">KV cache entries</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
```
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="pdf"}
```{=latex}
\begin{center}
\captionof{table}{Fill colors encode tensor roles.}
\label{tbl-fill-colors}
\rowcolors{2}{white}{gray!8}
\begin{tabular}{cl}
\toprule
Fill & Meaning \\
\midrule
\fcolorbox{stroke-neutral}{fill-neutral}{\hspace{0.7cm}\rule{0pt}{0.4cm}} & Input / activations (neutral) \\[4pt]
\fcolorbox{stroke-neutral}{fill-weight}{\hspace{0.7cm}\rule{0pt}{0.4cm}} & Weight matrices \\[4pt]
\fcolorbox{dim-d}{fill-query}{\hspace{0.7cm}\rule{0pt}{0.4cm}} & Queries ($\mathbf{Q}$) \\[4pt]
\fcolorbox{stroke-neutral}{fill-key}{\hspace{0.7cm}\rule{0pt}{0.4cm}} & Keys ($\mathbf{K}$) \\[4pt]
\fcolorbox{stroke-neutral}{fill-value}{\hspace{0.7cm}\rule{0pt}{0.4cm}} & Values ($\mathbf{V}$) \\[4pt]
\fcolorbox{stroke-neutral}{fill-attn}{\hspace{0.7cm}\rule{0pt}{0.4cm}} & Attention scores / weights \\[4pt]
\fcolorbox{stroke-neutral}{fill-kvcache}{\hspace{0.7cm}\rule{0pt}{0.4cm}} & KV cache entries \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}
```
:::


### Component colors

Architectural block diagrams use a separate set of fill colors to distinguish transformer components.

::: {.content-visible when-format="html"}
```{=html}
<table style="border-collapse: collapse; margin: 1em 0;">
<caption>Component colors in architectural block diagrams.</caption>
<thead>
<tr>
  <th style="padding: 8px 16px; border-bottom: 2px solid #333; text-align: center;">Color</th>
  <th style="padding: 8px 16px; border-bottom: 2px solid #333; text-align: left;">Component</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px; text-align: center;">
    <svg width="40" height="24"><rect x="0" y="0" width="40" height="24" rx="3" fill="#fce0e1" stroke="#333333" stroke-width="1"/></svg>
  </td>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px;">Embedding layers</td>
</tr>
<tr style="background-color: #f5f5f5;">
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px; text-align: center;">
    <svg width="40" height="24"><rect x="0" y="0" width="40" height="24" rx="3" fill="#f2f4c1" stroke="#333333" stroke-width="1"/></svg>
  </td>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px;">Layer normalization / Add & Norm</td>
</tr>
<tr>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px; text-align: center;">
    <svg width="40" height="24"><rect x="0" y="0" width="40" height="24" rx="3" fill="#ffe2bb" stroke="#333333" stroke-width="1"/></svg>
  </td>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px;">Self-attention blocks</td>
</tr>
<tr style="background-color: #f5f5f5;">
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px; text-align: center;">
    <svg width="40" height="24"><rect x="0" y="0" width="40" height="24" rx="3" fill="#c2e8f7" stroke="#333333" stroke-width="1"/></svg>
  </td>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px;">Feed-forward blocks</td>
</tr>
<tr>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px; text-align: center;">
    <svg width="40" height="24"><rect x="0" y="0" width="40" height="24" rx="3" fill="#dbdfef" stroke="#333333" stroke-width="1"/></svg>
  </td>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px;">Linear projections</td>
</tr>
<tr style="background-color: #f5f5f5;">
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px; text-align: center;">
    <svg width="40" height="24"><rect x="0" y="0" width="40" height="24" rx="3" fill="#cce7cf" stroke="#333333" stroke-width="1"/></svg>
  </td>
  <td style="padding: 8px 16px;">Softmax</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
```
:::

::: {.content-visible when-format="pdf"}
```{=latex}
\begin{center}
\captionof{table}{Component colors in architectural block diagrams.}
\label{tbl-component-colors}
\rowcolors{2}{white}{gray!8}
\begin{tabular}{cl}
\toprule
Color & Component \\
\midrule
\fcolorbox{stroke-dark}{comp-embed}{\hspace{0.7cm}\rule{0pt}{0.4cm}} & Embedding layers \\[4pt]
\fcolorbox{stroke-dark}{comp-norm}{\hspace{0.7cm}\rule{0pt}{0.4cm}} & Layer normalization / Add \& Norm \\[4pt]
\fcolorbox{stroke-dark}{comp-attn}{\hspace{0.7cm}\rule{0pt}{0.4cm}} & Self-attention blocks \\[4pt]
\fcolorbox{stroke-dark}{comp-ffn}{\hspace{0.7cm}\rule{0pt}{0.4cm}} & Feed-forward blocks \\[4pt]
\fcolorbox{stroke-dark}{comp-linear}{\hspace{0.7cm}\rule{0pt}{0.4cm}} & Linear projections \\[4pt]
\fcolorbox{stroke-dark}{comp-softmax}{\hspace{0.7cm}\rule{0pt}{0.4cm}} & Softmax \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}
```
:::


### Reading a data flow diagram

To illustrate how these conventions combine, here is how a typical tensor rectangle should be read:

- The **top and bottom edge colors** tell you the dimension that runs horizontally, the number of columns.
- The **left and right edge colors** tell you the dimension that runs vertically, the number of rows.
- The **shape label** (e.g., **([S]{.dim-s}, [D]{.dim-d})**) confirms the dimensions explicitly.
- The **fill color** helps disambiguate what kind of tensor it is (query, key, weight, etc.).

When two tensors share an edge color on a matching side, it means those dimensions are compatible for the operation that connects them --- a matrix multiply, a concatenation, or an elementwise operation. 
For matrix multiplcation, the number of columns on the lefthand tensor must match the number of rows on the righthand tensor.
For elementwise operations, usually all dimensions must match, unless one of the dimensions is 1, and it is broadcast to match the other tensor.
